title: Stash Internals, Partial Work, and Portable Patches · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs stash internals, partial work, and portable patches from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect stash commit structure, parents, refs, reflog, staged state, and untracked representation instead of treating a stash as an external bag.
Use pathspec, patch, staged, keep-index, include-untracked, and all controls while previewing secret and ignored-file consequences.
Choose apply, pop, branch, store, create, export, or import from collision risk, portability, and retention needs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G48 is a review in a disposable repository. only reviewed staged work should move while unstaged experiments remain visible. The learner must predict the stash records staged state and the working tree keeps unrelated unstaged paths, run git stash push --staged --message urgent-policy-wip, explain policy changes moved to stash@{0} while the experiment remains unstaged for inspection, test this changed condition: add an untracked credential fixture and reject include-untracked until secret review passes, and preserve this recovery boundary: create a named branch or bundle for valuable work before expiring the temporary stash. Competency probe: Create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.

Inspect the Gita Wip Recovery Policy trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create named durable refs or encrypted external backups for important work, verify restoration on a changed base, and expire temporary objects deliberately.

content.text: For Gita Wip Recovery Policy, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A stash is an appropriate durable backup and collaboration channel.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Wip Recovery Policy, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Gita Wip Recovery Policy record says work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve separate evidence from confidence.

For separate evidence from confidence, apply Gita Wip Recovery Policy without its stated preconditions, omit the work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Wip Recovery Policy for work-in-progress preservation and transfer ledger.
